claim: Using a scraper (reusable parser) instead of parsing each HTML page with LLM saves approximately 60-70% of tokens for multi-page scraping tasks
actionable: When scraping multiple pages, have agents build a reusable parser/scraper once, then execute it on all pages—this saves massive token overhead vs. parsing each page individually.
source_title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: Agents can maintain scrapers automatically—detecting selector changes, fixing broken data, and repairing pipelines without human intervention via scheduled loops
actionable: Set up scheduled agent loops (e.g., every 30 minutes) that validate scraped data and auto-repair broken selectors—enables 24/7 self-healing pipelines.
source_title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: Markdown extraction (text-only without HTML) is more token-efficient than full HTML parsing for agent data extraction tasks
actionable: Request 'scrape as markdown' from web APIs instead of full HTML when using agents—this reduces token consumption while preserving the data agents need.
source_title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: MCP with browser automation can mimic real human behavior (mouse movements, typing speed, clicks) to evade bot detection systems
actionable: When building scrapers that face bot detection, use browser MCPs that implement human-like behavior patterns (variable typing speed, mouse movement curves) to avoid blocks.
source_title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: Public data scraping is legal even if obtained through circumventing bot detection (ruling: public data remains public regardless of collection method)
actionable: For public data, verify website terms of service explicitly forbid scraping before assuming legality—legal precedent favors public data collection regardless of method if terms don't forbid it.
source_title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
